required_skills: Design System Architecture, Governance, and Multi-Team Adoption Strategy
Component Library Design with Variants, Properties, and Interactive States in Figma
Design Token Definition, Naming Conventions, and Multi-Theme Management
Typography Systems, Color Palette Architecture, and Spacing Scale Definition
Responsive and Adaptive Component Design across Breakpoints
Accessibility Standards Integration including WCAG 2.1 AA across All Components
Documentation Writing for Component Usage Guidelines and Pattern Libraries
Cross-functional Collaboration with Engineering to Achieve Code-Design Parity
Versioning, Changelog Management, and Design System Release Processes
preferred_skills: Proficiency in front-end code (HTML, CSS, React, or Web Components) to contribute directly to coded component libraries
Experience with design token pipeline tools such as Style Dictionary or Theo
Multi-brand or white-label design system architecture experience
DesignOps process design including tool stack governance and workflow automation
Icon system design and SVG optimization for scalable asset delivery
Dark mode, high-contrast mode, and multi-theme token architecture
Experience integrating design systems with Storybook for living documentation
Familiarity with analytics tooling to measure design system adoption and component usage
responsibilities: Architect, build, and maintain a scalable, multi-platform design system in Figma encompassing components, tokens, patterns, and guidelines that serve multiple product teams simultaneously
Define a comprehensive design token taxonomy covering color, typography, spacing, elevation, motion, and border-radius values, structured for multi-theme and multi-brand extensibility

Design exhaustive component specifications including all variants, states (default, hover, focus, active, disabled, error), responsive behaviors, and accessibility annotations for engineering implementation
Partner with front-end engineering teams to establish and maintain strict design-code parity, ensuring Figma components are mirrored accurately in the coded component library and Storybook
Author clear, structured component documentation including usage guidelines, do and don't examples, content guidelines, and accessibility requirements published in the team's design system portal
Establish design system governance processes including contribution workflows, design critique protocols, deprecation policies, and versioned release changelogs for cross-team transparency
Conduct regular component audits across product surfaces to identify inconsistencies, redundant patterns, and opportunities to consolidate or extend the system's component coverage
Manage the design token pipeline from design tool to code using tools such as Tokens Studio and Style Dictionary, ensuring tokens are correctly transformed and consumed across platforms
Lead onboarding sessions, design system office hours, and enablement workshops to drive adoption of the system by product designers, engineers, and content designers across the organization
Collect and analyze design system usage metrics, component adoption rates, and designer feedback to prioritize the system roadmap and demonstrate the system's impact on design velocity
